title: What Should You Charge？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: What Should You Charge？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: What should patients pay for dermal fillers if you ask someone how much should I pay for some snips on my hair and people would quickly realize that the snips are not what you should be paying for the advice and the styling and the overall result and that's essentially the skill …

segments.text: what should patients pay for dermal fillers if you ask someone how much should I pay for some snips on my hair and people would quickly realize that the snips are not what you should be paying for the advice and the styling and the overall result and that's essentially the skill of the hairdresser in this case and this is what we need to as clinicians get away from is we shouldn't be selling mils of dermal filler because I can promise you a mil in someone else's hands is very different to a mil in my hands and really what I'm selling is not the mill it's the skill and you need to get that across and hone your skills so that you're not talking about selling a mill of product and what I provide for my clients is an assessment a safety net great advice a long-term care artistic artistry a psychological approach to what's actually going to make them happy and someone to talk to I do all of this stuff I'm not just a mill of filler being held in the hand of sort of there could be anyone I'm providing a much

more holistic service and this is what I urge all of you to do is that you get away from charging for a mill and you start to charge for all the value that you can add to patients and you aim high and you start to add as much value as possible so that you actually enjoy your work more and you're not on the backfoot trying to compete on price

keyPoints.text: More holistic service and this is what I urge all of you to do is that you get away from charging for a mill and you start to charge for all the value that you can add to patients and you aim high and you start to add as…

transcript: what should patients pay for dermal fillers if you ask someone how much should I pay for some snips on my hair and people would quickly realize that the snips are not what you should be paying for the advice and the styling and the overall result and that's essentially the skill of the hairdresser in this case and this is what we need to as clinicians get away from is we shouldn't be selling mils of dermal filler because I can promise you a mil in someone else's hands is very different to a mil in my hands and really what I'm selling is not the mill it's the skill and you need to get that across and hone your skills so that you're not talking about selling a mill of product and what I provide for my clients is an assessment a safety net great advice a long-term care artistic artistry a psychological approach to what's actually going to make them happy and someone to talk to I do all of this stuff I'm not just a mill of filler being held in the hand of sort of there could be anyone I'm providing a much more holistic service and this is what I urge all of you to do is that you get away from charging for a mill and you start to charge for all the value that you can add to patients and you aim high and you start to add as much value as possible so that you actually enjoy your work more and you're not on the backfoot trying to compete on price
